title: andira inermis subsp. inermis
intro: Andira inermis subsp. inermis, also known as the 'Guacu' or 'Brazilian ironwood', is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The medicinal properties of Andira inermis subsp. inermis include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Andira inermis subsp. inermis can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to extract its medicinal compounds.
